skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide for planning, writing, organizing, and packaging reusable AI skills with clear instructions and supporting resources.
skill.summary: Create effective AI skills with structured instructions, resources, packaging guidance, and iteration workflows.
skill.author: BOM-98

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high and critical patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown examples, URLs, and ordinary prose rather than executable code. Human review found no malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection, but the skill does instruct agents to run relative scripts that are not included in the package and discusses .env handling. Publish with a warning and review any generated or workspace-local scripts before execution.

medium_findings.title: Relative Script Execution Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run scripts/init_skill.py and scripts/package_skill.py, but the reported file structure only contains SKILL.md. If followed in a workspace that contains different relative scripts, an agent could execute unintended local code. This is an instruction-level risk, not evidence of bundled malware.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct and specific, and the package does not include the referenced scripts. Exploitability depends on the agent execution environment and whether the user approves running workspace-local scripts.

medium_findings.title: Environment File Handling Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends that generated scripts read process.env and several .env locations. This can be legitimate for skill configuration, but it increases the chance that generated scripts access secrets unless authors add strict handling and avoid logging or transmitting values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env and process.env references are explicit, but no code in this package reads or exfiltrates secrets. The concern is future script behavior created from this guidance.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command detections are false positives caused by fenced examples, inline paths, and directory names in documentation. SKILL.md itself contains no executable Ruby, shell, Node, or Python code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review confirmed the flagged content is Markdown documentation and command examples. The only actionable concern is the separate instruction-level risk for relative script execution.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to public Claude documentation in the References section. They are not active network requests and do not receive local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plain Markdown reference links. No fetch, upload, webhook, or network-capable code exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptography on prose and Markdown lines, but no cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher, or security-sensitive algorithm is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review found skill authoring text, examples, and validation descriptions rather than cryptographic implementation. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar algorithm use.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections appear on guidance about avoiding duplication and limiting user questions. They do not instruct agents to inspect the host system or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are ordinary skill design guidance. No commands such as uname, whoami, env dumping, process listing, or network discovery are present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Script Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill gives relative commands for initialization and packaging scripts without bundling those scripts in the reported file structure.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is confirmed in the instructions. The risk is contextual because execution requires an agent or user to run the command in a workspace.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Script Secret Access Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells authors to make scripts read environment variables and .env files, which can expose secrets if generated scripts log, package, or transmit configuration values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The guidance is explicit but does not include secret exfiltration code. It should be treated as a design warning for generated scripts.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time rewriting instructions, examples, and setup steps for repeated AI workflows. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured process for designing reusable skills with clear resources and packaging steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides authors through concrete examples before writing a skill.
Shows when to use scripts, references, and assets as bundled resources.
Applies progressive disclosure to keep skill instructions concise.
Provides initialization, packaging, validation, and iteration workflow guidance.
Recommends metadata quality practices for automatic skill activation.
limitations: The package contains only SKILL.md and does not include the referenced helper scripts.
It is guidance material, not an automated validator or packaging tool.
Authors still need to supply domain knowledge, examples, and reusable resources.
Security review is still required for any scripts or assets added to a skill.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Plan and write a reusable skill for a repeated internal workflow, including resources and activation metadata.

use_cases.title: Refactor a Large Skill
use_cases.description: Move detailed material into references, scripts, or assets so the main instructions stay focused and efficient.

use_cases.title: Package a Skill for Sharing
use_cases.description: Prepare a skill for validation, packaging, testing, and iteration before distribution to other users.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a skill for this repeated workflow: [describe workflow]. Ask for the minimum examples needed before drafting the skill.

prompt_templates.title: Organize Skill Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill idea and recommend which content belongs in SKILL.md, references, scripts, or assets: [paste details].

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this existing skill for clarity, progressive disclosure, metadata quality, and missing reusable resources: [paste skill summary].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Distribution
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a release checklist for this skill, including validation, packaging, security review, examples, and iteration steps: [describe skill].

output_examples.input: I need a skill for generating weekly finance reports from the same data sources.
output_examples.output: A focused skill plan with trigger description, required references, reusable report templates, validation steps, and packaging checklist.

output_examples.input: My skill file is too long and repeats details from reference documents.
output_examples.output: A revised organization plan that keeps core workflow steps in the main file and moves detailed schemas into referenced documents.

output_examples.input: Review this skill before I share it with my team.
output_examples.output: A practical review covering metadata clarity, resource structure, missing tests, packaging readiness, and security review items.

best_practices: Start with concrete user examples before choosing resources or writing instructions.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed material into referenced files.
Review scripts, assets, and environment handling before packaging a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad skills that mix unrelated topics into one package.
Do not place secrets, private credentials, or sensitive data in skill files.
Do not rely on unbundled helper scripts without clear paths and review steps.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create reusable AI skills with structured instructions, metadata, references, scripts, assets, and packaging guidance.

faq.question: Does it include the helper scripts it mentions?
faq.answer: No. The scanned package contains only SKILL.md, so referenced scripts must be supplied and reviewed separately.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for reorganizing instructions, improving metadata, adding resources, and iterating after real use.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The file is mostly documentation, but it instructs agents to run relative scripts that are not bundled in the package.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing a skill?
faq.answer: Review bundled scripts, environment variable handling, references, assets, tests, and packaging output before distribution.
